my_role: Built an exact-scale miniature ice rink model; then disassembled a freezer to demonstrate the refrigeration cycle—extracted and safely disposed of freon while teaching how cooling pipes allow water to freeze.

Engineered a pneumatic arm inspired by stunt air ramps to launch basketballs with precision. Designed the system to achieve both free throws and 3-pointers with high repeatability by adjusting air pressure using plywood and steel construction.
Wrote a Python script to control 7 3D printers simultaneously; converting MIDI files into stepper motor movements that generated acoustic music from the printer mechanics.

Modified a cricket ball with sand filling to act as a deadblow hammer; eliminating bounce. Built a remote-controlled solenoid stump ejector system triggered by button press. Integrated 18650 batteries into the stump's confined space and designed polycarbonate stumps to prevent shattering during normal play.

Spent 3 months iterating an Edison-inspired phonograph design based on academic research. Designed everything in Fusion 360 with meticulous precision; incorporating 3D printing; woodworking; and ACME lead screws chosen over threaded rod for superior straightness and accuracy.

Built a helmet water fountain using dual peristaltic pumps offset 30 degrees out of phase to overcome pressure loss and sputtering over a 30-foot distance. Integrated the pumping system into a water fountain dispenser for a visually striking effect.

Engineered an airbag-powered ping pong ball launching system across 30 coordinated trashcans. Tested multiple launch mechanisms before settling on airbags with cinder block weights and cardboard impact dampers. Built a relay matrix system with precise timing/firing diagrams to synchronize all 30 trashcans despite limited relay availability.

Designed control systems for 40 air cannons filled with confetti; using a relay matrix coordination approach. Built timing and firing diagrams to synchronize all 40 pneumatic cannons for a coordinated celebration effect.

Conducted in-depth research into sonar imaging systems; ROV/AUV operations; and underwater exploration techniques. Synthesized technical knowledge about submerged site investigation; acoustic systems; and underwater logistics; then relayed findings and insights to Mark and the engineering team.

Researched rocket motor specifications and physics; then created mathematical simulations to predict swing loop dynamics under different thrust configurations. Designed and manufactured precision mounting points for rocket motors while implementing comprehensive safety protocols.
Fabricated wooden sign stands with real-time writing surfaces for video production. Handled on-set support and assisted with video execution.

Created multiple physics demonstration builds: designed and 3D printed an ellipse with interior mirror film lining and motorized laser holder to demonstrate focal point reflection principles; designed and built a 6-foot scaled longboard with wheels and grip tape; rigged a 6-foot fan on a boat with a sail and camping batteries to test self-propulsion via blown sail.

Designed a quick-swap aluminum extrusion rail mount system enabling three interchangeable wheelchair modes: (1) Party mode with motorized disco ball; (2) Car horn mode activated by button press; (3) Nerf battle mode with dual large nerf guns mounted on servos that swing up over shoulders with auto-firing. Integrated all systems to the wheelchair's 48V onboard battery with voltage step-down converters for each device.

First project working with Mark Rober's team. Provided on-set support and learned about discrete mechanical systems; timing mechanisms; and solenoid-based control systems used in the arcade game-winning device.

Led manufacturing and assembly of glitter bomb boxes at scale once design was finalized. Installed hidden cameras in all bait vehicles to capture thief footage. Designed and implemented a real-time alert system using Twilio API that notifies when a box loses connection; enabling pre-theft box resets. Managed production logistics and fleet-wide equipment maintenance.
Led mass production and assembly of glitter bomb devices. Conducted hands-on quality control testing of every unit. Managed charging infrastructure and logistics for the fleet of devices.

Retrofitted a Roomba to pick up nerf balls using a 3D printed acorn picker holder without damaging the original device. Built a Simon Says robot using Raspberry Pi with a camera module for color detection. Integrated servos with drum sticks and a mechanical hand that detects when lights turn on and slaps the corresponding color. Programmed sequence learning logic enabling the robot to remember patterns and beat the game.

Designed mini M&M vibration candy dispensers featuring a vibration motor activated by button press; with batteries miniaturized to fit the small tube form factor. Received 20 broken silly string machines; reverse-engineered and retrofitted all units to working condition—each containing silly string; a candy bowl spinner; and horn triggered by lid opening. Field-tested all 40 units for reliability.

Built a reaction wheel demonstration system to teach conservation of angular momentum. Designed and 3D printed the box enclosure. Integrated a BLDC motor with a carefully balanced acrylic plate; strategically placed screws to adjust moment of inertia; and controlled the system using an RC controller. Successfully demonstrated precise 90-degree; 180-degree; and arbitrary angle rotations to show any desired orientation.
Conducted extensive testing and validation of Mark's laser and lightning segments. Performed quality assurance testing across multiple experimental setups to ensure reliable performance.
Conducted extensive testing and validation of Mark's lava and laser segments. Performed quality assurance testing across multiple experimental setups to ensure reliable performance.

Built all electronics and Arduino sensor integration for an interactive crow engagement system. Designed and programmed custom puzzle logic enabling the crow to interact with mechanical challenges through sensor feedback and automated responses.

Designed and machined a custom aluminum fan blade to attach to a whiffle ball bat; significantly increasing the striking surface area. Precision CNC work to create an expanded fan-like blade that enhanced the bat's contact area and demonstrated aerodynamic effects on whiffle ball flight.

Built a fully waterproof boat from raincoat material using rubber cement as the primary adhesive. Designed and tested the construction to recreate the materials and techniques used in the famous Alcatraz prison escape; demonstrating material science and historical engineering.

Designed and built a massive density demonstration wall—possibly the world's largest—to visually demonstrate how different liquids have different densities. Conducted FEA (Finite Element Analysis) to calculate structural integrity and determine the thinnest acrylic that could safely withstand the pressure. Optimized material selection to minimize thickness while ensuring safety.

Engineered and integrated a reverse osmosis (RO) water system to demonstrate clean water purification technology. Designed the system for efficiency and sustainability; showcasing how RO filtration can produce clean drinking water.
